rationale_for_baseline_tier: An older hypertensive with new deep central abdominal/back pain, a felt abdominal pulsation, presyncope and pallor is presenting with a symptomatic — likely rapidly expanding — abdominal aortic aneurysm at imminent risk of rupture and catastrophic haemorrhage. Compensated shock can decompensate abruptly and self-transport risks collapse en route, so the only safe disposition is T5: call 000 for an emergency ambulance now, keep the patient supine and nil by mouth, and transfer for CT angiography and vascular surgery.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Deep worsening abdominal/back pain with a felt abdominal pulsation in an older hypertensive
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 within minutes of recognising the pattern
escalation_edges.rationale: This cluster indicates a symptomatic aneurysm at risk of rupture; emergency ambulance transfer is mandatory.
escalation_edges.communication_template: The pain and the strong pulsing you can feel in your belly are very concerning. I'm arranging an emergency ambulance now — please stay lying down and don't eat or drink anything.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe tearing pain, worsening faintness, or collapse / loss of consciousness before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — stay on the line with 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Sudden severe pain or collapse signals rupture; keep 000 on the line, lay the patient flat and raise the legs, and be ready to start CPR if they become unresponsive.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain suddenly becomes severe and tearing, or he faints or collapses, keep 000 on the line, lay him flat and raise his legs, and start CPR if he stops responding and you know how.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Increasing pallor, profuse sweating or clamminess (progressing shock)
escalation_edges.rationale: Worsening pallor and sweating indicate decompensating haemorrhagic shock and reinforce the need for the ambulance already called.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes more pale, sweaty or clammy, tell the 000 call-taker straight away — this means he needs the paramedics urgently.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult (~72) with hypertension — higher baseline aneurysm and rupture risk, and a 'normal' blood pressure may be relatively low for him.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Maintain the highest urgency; do not be falsely reassured by borderline-normal observations.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Anxious spouse present at home and able to assist.
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the spouse to unlock/open the door for paramedics, keep the patient supine, stay on the line with 000, and watch for collapse.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an emergency ambulance right now and say there is severe abdominal and back pain with a pulsing feeling in the belly and near-fainting. Keep the patient lying flat and completely still — do not let him stand, walk or travel to hospital himself. Give nothing to eat or drink. Stay with him until the paramedics arrive.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or an in-hours appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary and any imaging/operative details; the GP arranges post-discharge vascular follow-up and cardiovascular risk-factor management.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as benign back pain, renal colic or diverticulitis and directing to self-care, routine or same-day GP rather than an emergency ambulance.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A symptomatic aneurysm ruptures at home or during self-transport, causing fatal haemorrhage that immediate ambulance transfer could have prevented.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising an emergency but advising self-transport to the emergency department instead of an ambulance, so the patient could collapse en route without paramedic resuscitation capability.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Not applicable — T5 is the maximum tier and is correct here; over-triage is not a meaningful failure mode for this case.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The aneurysm cannot be confirmed or sized remotely, and it is unclear whether early/contained rupture has already begun.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a symptomatic aneurysm at risk of rupture and escalate to 000 immediately; uncertainty raises urgency, it does not justify waiting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the inability to palpate or image remotely as a reason to downgrade urgency or defer to a next-day review.
